title: tauscheria lasiocarpa var. gymnocarpa
intro: Tauscheria lasiocarpa var. gymnocarpa, also known as mountain cherry or bearberry, is a low-growing, deciduous shrub native to high-altitude mountainous regions in North America. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its astringent and antiseptic properties, making it useful in treating various health issues such as diarrhea, skin infections, and urinary tract problems. The health benefits of Tauscheria lasiocarpa var. gymnocarpa include its ability to boost immune function, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, and can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
